metadata.description: Curated resource database for the AIREX Conversational Repository

resources.title: AI Ethics in Academic Assessment: Course Syllabus
resources.institution: University of Texas at El Paso
resources.description: A senior-level course examining ethical implications of AI in academic settings, with a focus on assessment integrity, algorithmic bias, and responsible deployment in educational contexts.

readings.title: Weapons of Math Destruction
readings.author: Cathy O'Neil

readings.title: The Ethical Algorithm
readings.author: Michael Kearns and Aaron Roth

readings.title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
readings.author: Emily Bender, Timnit Gebru et al.

readings.title: AI and Academic Integrity: A Framework for Ethical Use
readings.author: UNESCO

readings.title: Teaching AI Ethics: A Case-Based Approach
readings.author: Shannon Vallor

resources.title: Generative AI Policy Template for Universities
resources.description: A comprehensive template for institutional AI policies covering acceptable use, data privacy, student rights, and faculty guidelines. Includes sections on generative AI in coursework, research ethics, and administrative applications.

content_sections.summary: Defines permitted and restricted uses of generative AI tools by students, faculty, and staff. Includes specific guidance on when AI-generated content must be disclosed.
Framework for faculty to communicate AI tool policies in syllabi. Includes three tiers: AI-prohibited, AI-assisted (with disclosure), and AI-integrated assignments.
Requirements for protecting student data when using AI tools. Prohibits entering personally identifiable information into public AI systems.
Mandates AI literacy training for all faculty within two semesters. Includes recommended workshops and certification pathways.

resources.title: AI-Enhanced Teaching Methods: Faculty Workshop Curriculum

contributor.name: Dr. Ana Lucia Vargas
contributor.role: Director, Center for Teaching and Learning

resources.description: A 6-session faculty workshop series designed to help instructors integrate AI tools into their teaching practice. Covers prompt engineering, AI-assisted assessment design, and pedagogical strategies for AI-augmented classrooms.

sessions.title: Understanding AI in Higher Education
sessions.description: Overview of current AI tools and their capabilities. Hands-on exploration of ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini in academic contexts.

sessions.title: Redesigning Assignments for the AI Era
sessions.description: Strategies for creating assessments that leverage AI rather than compete with it. Process-based evaluation methods.

sessions.title: AI-Assisted Feedback and Grading
sessions.description: Using AI to provide formative feedback. Maintaining academic integrity while streamlining evaluation workflows.

sessions.title: Prompt Engineering for Educators
sessions.description: Developing effective prompts for content creation, lesson planning, and student support. Building reusable prompt libraries.

sessions.title: Equity and Access in AI-Enhanced Learning
sessions.description: Addressing the digital divide. Ensuring AI tools are accessible and beneficial across diverse student populations.

sessions.title: Building Your AI Teaching Portfolio
sessions.description: Participants develop and present an AI-enhanced course module. Peer review and refinement session.

readings.title: Teaching with AI: A Practical Guide
readings.author: Jose Antonio Bowen and C. Edward Watson

readings.title: AI in Education: Promises and Implications for Teaching and Learning
readings.author: Charles Fadel et al.

resources.title: Computing Infrastructure Assessment Checklist for AI Readiness
resources.description: A comprehensive checklist for evaluating institutional computing infrastructure readiness for AI workloads. Covers hardware, networking, cloud services, data storage, security, and staffing requirements.

items: Secure data enclaves for sensitive datasets

resources.title: AI Research Partnership Playbook
resources.description: Step-by-step guide for establishing AI research partnerships between universities and industry. Covers proposal development, IP agreements, student involvement, and joint publication strategies.

content_sections.summary: Methods for identifying industry partners aligned with institutional research strengths. Includes outreach templates and evaluation criteria.
Key terms to negotiate in AI research partnerships including IP rights, data sharing, publication timelines, and student involvement.
Best practices for coordinating research across institutional and corporate teams. Communication protocols and milestone tracking.
Frameworks for involving graduate and undergraduate students in industry-partnered AI research. Internship pipelines and co-op models.

resources.title: Responsible AI Curriculum: Graduate Seminar Syllabus
resources.description: An interdisciplinary graduate seminar exploring responsible AI development and deployment. Students analyze real-world case studies, develop ethical impact assessments, and create institutional responsibility frameworks.

readings.title: Atlas of AI: Power, Politics, and the Planetary Costs of Artificial Intelligence
readings.author: Kate Crawford

readings.title: Race After Technology: Abolitionist Tools for the New Jim Code
readings.author: Ruha Benjamin

readings.title: Artificial Unintelligence: How Computers Misunderstand the World
readings.author: Meredith Broussard

readings.title: Ethical and Social Risks of Harm from Language Models
readings.author: DeepMind Ethics Team

readings.title: A Framework for Ethical AI in Education
readings.author: IEEE Education Society

readings.title: Algorithms of Oppression
readings.author: Safiya Umoja Noble

resources.title: AI Governance Committee Charter Template
resources.description: Template for establishing an institutional AI governance committee. Defines membership, scope, decision-making authority, and reporting structures for managing AI policy across the institution.

content_sections.summary: Recommended membership includes faculty senate representatives, IT leadership, student government, legal counsel, and community advisory members. Minimum 9, maximum 15 members with staggered terms.
Committee reviews and approves AI tool procurement, develops use policies, handles complaints and concerns, and advises leadership on AI strategy.
Monthly meetings with quarterly reports to the Board. Annual AI readiness report published publicly. Emergency convening protocol for urgent AI-related issues.

content_sections.section: Review Process for New AI Tools
content_sections.summary: 30-day review cycle for proposed AI tool adoptions. Includes privacy impact assessment, equity review, and pilot testing requirements.

resources.title: AI Literacy for All Students: General Education Module
resources.description: A 4-week AI literacy module designed to be embedded in any general education course. No technical prerequisites. Students learn to evaluate AI outputs, understand data bias, and use AI tools responsibly in their discipline.

sessions.title: What is AI and How Does It Work?
sessions.description: Demystifying AI for non-technical students. Interactive demos showing how language models, image generators, and recommendation systems function at a conceptual level.

sessions.title: Evaluating AI Outputs: Critical Thinking in the AI Era
sessions.description: Hands-on exercises where students compare AI-generated content with verified sources. Identifying hallucinations, bias, and limitations.

sessions.title: AI and Your Discipline
sessions.description: Discipline-specific exploration of how AI is used in nursing, business, education, engineering, and arts. Students research AI applications in their field.

sessions.title: Being an Ethical AI User
sessions.description: Understanding data privacy, intellectual property, and responsible use. Students create personal AI use guidelines for their academic work.

readings.title: AI for Everyone (Coursera)
readings.author: Andrew Ng

readings.title: What Is ChatGPT Doing... and Why Does It Work?
readings.author: Stephen Wolfram

resources.title: Data Governance Framework for AI-Ready Institutions
resources.description: A comprehensive data governance framework designed for universities preparing to scale AI initiatives. Addresses data quality, access controls, metadata standards, and ethical data use policies.

content_sections.summary: Four-tier classification system: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted. Each tier has specific AI use permissions and access protocols.
Requirements for data used in AI systems: completeness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency. Quarterly audits and data steward responsibilities.
Role-based access control for AI training data. Institutional review board approval required for AI research using student data.
Guidelines for training AI models with institutional data. Bias testing requirements, demographic impact assessments, and transparency obligations.

resources.title: AI Research Lab Startup Guide for Mid-Size Universities
resources.description: Practical guide for establishing an AI research lab at universities without large existing compute infrastructure. Covers cloud-first strategies, grant writing tips, faculty recruitment, and cross-departmental collaboration models.

content_sections.summary: How to leverage AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud academic programs to access GPU computing without capital investment. Includes cost estimation templates.
Curated list of NSF, NIH, DOE, and DOD funding programs for AI research at primarily undergraduate institutions. Includes timeline and proposal tips.
Models for building AI research teams across CS, Engineering, Social Sciences, and Health Sciences. Shared lab spaces and equipment pooling strategies.
Creating research pathways from freshman year through graduate school. REU programs, mentorship models, and conference presentation support.

resources.title: Integrating AI into Nursing Education: Case Studies and Readings
resources.institution: University of Texas at San Antonio

contributor.name: Dr. Rebecca Torres
contributor.department: College of Health, Community and Policy

resources.description: A course examining how AI is transforming healthcare delivery and nursing practice. Students evaluate clinical decision support systems, AI diagnostic tools, and patient communication platforms.

readings.title: The AI Revolution in Medicine
readings.author: Peter Lee, Carey Goldberg, and Isaac Kohane

readings.title: Artificial Intelligence in Nursing: Challenges and Opportunities
readings.author: Journal of Nursing Scholarship

readings.title: Bias in Clinical Algorithms: A Systematic Review
readings.author: NEJM AI

readings.title: AI-Powered Patient Communication: Benefits and Risks
readings.author: WHO Digital Health Technical Brief

resources.title: Equity Impact Assessment Guide for AI Tool Adoption

contributor.name: Dr. Jennifer Okonkwo
contributor.role: Director, Center for Equity and Inclusion
contributor.department: Division of Equity, Justice and Inclusive Excellence

resources.description: A step-by-step guide for conducting equity impact assessments before adopting AI tools on campus. Includes rubrics, stakeholder interview templates, and decision matrices for evaluating AI tools through an equity lens.

content_sections.summary: 12-question screening tool to evaluate potential equity impacts before piloting any AI tool. Flags high-risk tools for deeper review.
Method for identifying which student and staff populations will be affected by an AI tool. Includes templates for focus groups with underrepresented groups.
Technical and non-technical approaches to testing AI tools for demographic bias. Includes sample test cases and evaluation criteria.
Post-deployment equity monitoring including usage analytics by demographic, complaint tracking, and quarterly bias audits.

resources.title: AI Strategy Development Roadmap for University Leadership
resources.description: A roadmap for university presidents and provosts to develop institutional AI strategies. Covers stakeholder engagement, resource allocation, timeline planning, and success metrics for AI integration across campus.

content_sections.summary: Internal audit of current AI usage, faculty survey, infrastructure assessment, and peer institution benchmarking.
Forming the AI strategy committee, drafting vision and goals, identifying quick wins, and establishing the governance framework.
Launching 3-5 pilot projects across different units. Dedicated funding allocation, faculty champion program, and student feedback loops.
Expanding successful pilots, building permanent AI support infrastructure, integrating AI into strategic plan, and establishing industry partnerships.

resources.title: Cloud Computing for AI: Budget Planning Template
resources.description: A budget planning spreadsheet and guide for estimating cloud computing costs for AI workloads. Includes cost models for AWS, Azure, and GCP with academic discount calculations.

content_sections.summary: Overview of AWS Educate, Azure for Education, Google for Education credits. Application timelines and renewal processes.
Spot instances for training, right-sizing GPU selection, auto-scaling for inference, and shared compute pools across departments.

resources.title: Undergraduate AI Research Experience Program Design
resources.institution: University of Texas Rio Grande Valley

contributor.name: Dr. Sofia Delgado
contributor.department: Office of Engaged Scholarship and Learning

resources.description: Complete program design for undergraduate AI research experiences. Includes recruitment strategies for underrepresented students, mentorship models, project templates, and conference presentation preparation.

content_sections.summary: 10-week summer program plus academic year continuation. 15 students per cohort, paired with faculty and graduate student mentors.
Targeted outreach to first-generation and underrepresented students. Portfolio-based application process that values potential over prior experience.
Starter project ideas across AI subfields: NLP for community health, computer vision for agriculture, ML for student success prediction.
Conference presentation coaching, research poster workshops, graduate school application support, and industry networking events.

resources.title: AI in Business Education: Course Redesign Guide
resources.description: Guide for redesigning traditional business courses to incorporate AI tools and concepts. Includes case studies on AI in marketing, finance, operations, and human resources.

readings.title: Competing in the Age of AI
readings.author: Marco Iansiti and Karim Lakhani

readings.title: Prediction Machines: The Simple Economics of Artificial Intelligence
readings.author: Ajay Agrawal, Joshua Gans, and Avi Goldfarb

readings.title: Human + Machine: Reimagining Work in the Age of AI
readings.author: Paul Daugherty and H. James Wilson

resources.title: Student AI Acceptable Use Agreement Template
resources.description: A student-facing agreement template that clearly communicates expectations for AI use in academic work. Written in accessible language. Includes a student acknowledgment form and FAQ section.

content_sections.summary: Plain-language explanation of generative AI tools, how they work, and their limitations. Written at an accessible reading level for diverse student populations.
Clear guidelines on three categories: assignments where AI is encouraged, where it is permitted with disclosure, and where it is not allowed.
Citation formats for AI-assisted work following APA, MLA, and Chicago styles. Includes examples and a quick-reference card.
Answers to common student questions: Can I use AI for brainstorming? What if my professor has different rules? How do I know if my use is ethical?

resources.title: AI-Powered Student Success Early Warning System
resources.description: Case study documenting the development and deployment of an AI-based early warning system that identifies students at risk of academic difficulty. Covers model development, ethical safeguards, intervention design, and outcome data.

content_sections.summary: 25% first-year attrition rate despite existing advising programs. Traditional early warning systems relied on mid-term grades, missing early behavioral indicators.
Random forest model using LMS engagement data, course registration patterns, and financial aid status. Explicit exclusion of race, ethnicity, and zip code. Annual bias audits by external reviewers.
Three-tier intervention: automated resource emails (Tier 1), advisor outreach (Tier 2), intensive case management (Tier 3). Students can opt out at any time.
12% reduction in first-year attrition. 89% of flagged students who engaged with Tier 2 interventions completed the semester. No significant disparities in flag rates across demographic groups.
